name: Cocoa Meringue Cake with Raspberry Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 200 degrees.
2. Cover two cookie sheets with foil and draw two 9-inch circles on it.
3. Whisk the egg whites and 1 teaspoon lemon juice until they form soft peaks.
4. Gradually add 3/4 cup sugar, a spoonful at a time, and beat constantly until
the sugar is fully incorporated and the meringue is stiff and shiny.
5. Divide the meringue between the two circles, spreading to the edges.
6. Bake for 4 hours, then turn off the oven. Leave in the oven until the
meringues are cold. You can make these ahead of time and store in a
cardboard box.
7. In a saucepan, combine the margarine and cocoa and cook over low heat for
3 minutes, stirring constantly. Do not let it burn.
8. Pour hot milk into the cocoa mixture, stirring constantly with a wire whisk.
9. Add 1/2 cup of sugar and coffee granules, and simmer, stirring constantly,
until thick.
10. Remove from heat and add vanilla; mix well. Allow sauce to cool, then
refrigerate for at least 2 hours.
11. When ready to use, beat the mixture until fluffy and lighter in color.
12. Spread on one meringue and sandwich the two meringues together.
13. To serve, cut the meringue cakes into wedges and spoon about 2 tablespoons
of raspberry sauce over each wedge. Garnish as desired.
Original recipe-331 calories per serving, 23 g fat. Revised version-180 calories,
4 g fat. Jo Merrill--personal files
